data.title: UK Fuel Retailers Maintain Margins Amid Iran Conflict
data.description: Fuel retailers in the United Kingdom largely maintained their profit margins during the initial impact of the conflict involving Iran, according to the UK's antitrust watchdog. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that despite the geopolitical tensions, there was no significant change in the pricing strategies of these retailers. The watchdog's observation comes amid concerns about potential price hikes due to the instability in the region. The report suggests that the retailers' decision to keep margins steady helped mitigate immediate economic disruptions for consumers.

data.title: Tokenisation to Surge 5,600% to $2 Trillion by 2028, Says Standard Chartered
data.description: Tokenisation is projected to grow by 5,600% to reach a $2 trillion market by 2028, according to Standard Chartered. The bank attributes this growth to the expansion of decentralised finance (DeFi) lending via stablecoins, which facilitate the migration of real-world assets like stocks and bonds onchain. Geoffrey Kendrick, global head of digital assets research at Standard Chartered, highlighted the resilience of the DeFi sector despite recent challenges, such as the $300 million exploit of KelpDAO. Kendrick argues that rapid stabilisation efforts and structural upgrades bolster the long-term prospects for DeFi banking and stablecoin liquidity, supporting the projected market growth.

data.title: Moderna's Stock Rises Over 10% Pre-Market as Q1 Revenue Exceeds Expectations
data.description: Moderna's stock experienced a significant increase of over 10% in pre-market trading. According to Jin10, the company's first-quarter revenue surpassed market expectations, contributing to the stock's upward movement.

data.title: ECB Official Mahrouf: Inflation Expectations Require Close Monitoring
data.description: European Central Bank Governing Council member Mahrouf emphasized the importance of closely monitoring inflation expectations. According to Jin10, Mahrouf highlighted the need to watch for any signs of de-anchoring in these expectations, which could have significant implications for monetary policy.

data.title: ECB's Makhlouf: Wage-Driven Inflation Effects May Take Longer to Emerge
data.description: European Central Bank Governing Council member Gabriel Makhlouf has indicated that due to the staggered nature of wage setting, the secondary inflation effects potentially arising from wage channels may take a longer time to manifest. According to Jin10, Makhlouf's comments highlight the complexities involved in predicting inflationary trends linked to wage adjustments.

data.title: ExxonMobil Shares Rise 1.8% Pre-Market as Q1 Profits Exceed Expectations
data.description: ExxonMobil's stock saw a 1.8% increase in pre-market trading following the announcement of its first-quarter profits surpassing expectations. According to Jin10, the oil giant's financial performance has been positively received by investors, contributing to the uptick in share price.

data.title: ExxonMobil and Chevron Reject White House's Call for Increased Production Amid Energy Crisis
data.description:
ExxonMobil and Chevron have declined the White House's request to increase oil production, maintaining their current strategies despite the U.S. government's efforts to address the most severe energy crisis in decades. According to Jin10, ExxonMobil CFO Neil Hansen stated that the company's strategy in the Permian Basin, a major U.S. oil and gas region, remains unchanged. Chevron CFO Eimear Bonner echoed this sentiment, noting that the crisis has not prompted any changes to their plans.

The ongoing conflict in Iran has significantly reduced oil output in the Gulf region, impacting refining operations in the Middle East and beyond, potentially exacerbating global inflation. In response, the U.S. government has released strategic petroleum reserves and urged the industry to increase drilling activities. However, these two American energy giants continue to adhere to their pre-crisis strategies.

Hansen remarked, "We don't see a need to accelerate because our production is already high and operating at full capacity. While we have considered the possibility of expanding production, there are indeed constraints." Bonner added, "We could increase production in the Permian, but that's not our strategy. Our focus is on increasing free cash flow, not production. You can't expect us to drastically change plans due to an eight-week supply disruption."

These statements were made as both companies reported their first-quarter earnings on Friday.

data.title: ExxonMobil and Chevron Resist Pressure to Increase Oil Production
data.description: ExxonMobil and Chevron have resisted pressure from U.S. President Donald Trump to increase oil production, according to Odaily. ExxonMobil stated that the closure of the Strait of Hormuz throughout the quarter is expected to result in a 3% decrease in global product solutions throughput in the second quarter compared to the fourth quarter of 2025.

data.title: ExxonMobil defies pressure from Trump and refuses to increase oil production.
data.description: According to the Financial Times, ExxonMobil (XOM.N) and Chevron (CVX.N) withstood pressure from US President Trump and refused to increase oil production. (Jinshi)

data.title: MENA Region Faces Potential Disruption Amid Iran Conflict
data.description: The ongoing conflict involving Iran may lead to increased demand for on-chain settlement in the MENA region, according to NS3.AI. A potential closure of the Strait of Hormuz poses a threat to approximately 20% of the global oil supply, highlighting the geopolitical risks in the area. Additionally, Saudi Arabia has suspended marine vessel documentation requirements for 30 days, further indicating regional instability. TOKEN2049 Dubai's decision to postpone its event to 2027 also reflects the uncertainty affecting the region.

data.title: Chevron Surpasses Profit Forecasts Amid Rising Oil Prices and Hess Acquisition
data.description: Chevron has reported profits that exceeded expectations, driven by increased oil and natural gas prices and additional supplies from its acquisition of Hess. Bloomberg posted on X that these gains helped offset production disruptions caused by the ongoing conflict in Iran. The company's financial performance highlights the impact of strategic acquisitions and market conditions on its bottom line.

data.title: SpaceX's Starship Project Exceeds $15 Billion in Spending
data.description: SpaceX's IPO registration documents reveal that the company's expenditure on the Starship project has surpassed $15 billion as the upgraded prototype's debut approaches. According to Jin10, this significant investment underscores SpaceX's commitment to advancing its ambitious space exploration goals.

data.title: SpaceX Plans to Launch Next-Gen Starlink V3 Satellites in Late 2026
data.description: SpaceX has revealed plans to launch its first batch of next-generation Starlink V3 satellites in the second half of 2026, according to Jin10. The company's IPO registration documents indicate that these satellites will be more powerful than previous versions, aiming to enhance global internet connectivity.

data.title: AI Agents Empower Retail Traders in Financial Markets
data.description: Retail traders are increasingly utilizing AI agents to manage their investments across equities, cryptocurrencies, and prediction markets. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the trend of individuals leveraging artificial intelligence to automate trading decisions. This shift is driven by the desire for more efficient and informed trading strategies, as AI can analyze vast amounts of data and execute trades at optimal times. The adoption of AI in trading is reshaping how retail investors approach the market, offering them tools previously accessible only to institutional investors. As AI technology continues to evolve, its role in financial markets is expected to expand, providing retail traders with enhanced capabilities to navigate complex market dynamics.

data.title: Citi Forecasts Biannual Rate Hikes by Bank of Japan Starting June
data.description: Citi has projected that the Bank of Japan will begin implementing interest rate hikes approximately every six months starting in June. According to Jin10, this forecast suggests a shift in monetary policy by Japan's central bank, potentially impacting economic conditions and financial markets.

data.title: U.S. President Faces Challenges in Iran Conflict
data.description: Bloomberg posted on X that U.S. President Donald Trump is encountering difficulties in the ongoing conflict with Iran, which is increasingly resembling a quagmire. As the situation becomes more complex, there is speculation that the president might seek alternative strategies or take risks in other areas to offset the challenges faced in Iran. This development comes amid broader geopolitical tensions and strategic considerations.

data.title: S&P 500 and Nasdaq Hit All-Time Highs as Oil Surges to $126 Before Slumping; Alphabet Jumps 10%, Meta Falls 9%
data.description:
Key Takeaways
The S&P 500 jumped 1% and the Nasdaq added 0.9%, both reaching all-time highs, driven by strong corporate earnings and resilient US GDP growth of 2.0% in Q1 2026Brent crude spiked to $126 per barrel -- a four-year high -- before slumping 3.4% to close at $114.01 as initial Middle East escalation fears easedAlphabet surged 10% on strong AI-driven earnings; Meta fell 8.6% on concerns over heavy AI spending; Apple beat forecasts after the bell with shares up 4.7% in after-hours tradingThe Fed, ECB, and Bank of England all held rates unchanged; the ECB warned that war-related energy disruptions have "intensified" risks to eurozone growth and inflationUS Q1 2026 GDP grew at an annualized rate of 2.0%, defying fears of a near-term slowdown despite cooling consumer spending
US equity markets surged to record highs on Thursday as strong corporate earnings and resilient economic growth outweighed an extraordinary intraday spike in oil prices that briefly pushed Brent crude to $126 per barrel -- its highest level in four years -- before a sharp reversal closed the session with crude sharply lower.
The S&P 500 gained 1% and the Nasdaq Composite added 0.9%, both reaching all-time highs as investor optimism around tech earnings and AI-driven corporate profit growth overcame the morning's geopolitical anxiety. European markets also closed higher, taking their cue from the broadly positive US tech earnings backdrop.
Oil's Wild Session
The day's most dramatic price action came in energy markets. Brent crude surged to $126 per barrel in early trading -- its highest level since 2022 -- after President Trump warned that the US naval blockade of Iranian ports could last months and reports emerged that he would be briefed on potential fresh military strike options. The combination of escalation fears and the expiry of monthly futures contracts amplified the move.
The rally proved short-lived. Markets calmed as the initial panic subsided, and Brent closed down 3.4% at $114.01. "Fears about escalation in the conflict between the US and Iran fueled the initial move higher before the market calmed down," said Kathleen Brooks, research director at XTB. Despite the intraday reversal, Brent remains significantly elevated relative to its pre-conflict levels before US-Israel strikes targeting Iran began on February 28.
Earnings Drive the Record Rally
The equity market's record-setting session was powered by a split tech earnings picture that nonetheless left the bulls in control. Alphabet -- Google's parent company -- surged 10% as investors applauded the firm's successful pivot to artificial intelligence and solid revenue performance across its major divisions. Apple reported after the closing bell with earnings that beat forecasts on strong iPhone demand, sending shares up 4.7% in after-hours trading.
Meta was the session's major disappointment, falling 8.6% amid investor concern over the scale of its artificial intelligence capital expenditure commitments -- a reminder that AI spending enthusiasm has limits when it comes at the cost of near-term profitability.
Angelo Kourkafas of Edward Jones attributed the broader market strength to corporate profit resilience. "A lot of that comes down to corporate profits," he said, adding that US GDP data "continues to defy fears of a near-term slowdown."
GDP Beats, Consumer Spending Cools
The macro backdrop supported the bullish equity narrative. The US Commerce Department reported Thursday that the economy grew at an annualized rate of 2.0% in the first quarter of 2026 -- a figure that defied expectations of sharper slowdown given the geopolitical and inflationary headwinds of the period. A surge in AI-related investment was identified as a key growth driver, though consumer spending cooled during the quarter.
Central Banks Hold as Europe Warns
Thursday also brought a coordinated hold from three major central banks. The European Central Bank and Bank of England both kept rates unchanged, following the Federal Reserve's hold on Wednesday. The ECB issued a warning that risks to eurozone growth and inflation have "intensified" due to the war's impact on global energy supplies, while the Bank of England cut its UK growth forecast. Eurozone GDP data released Thursday showed the bloc's economy grew just 0.1% in Q1 2026 -- a near-stagnation reading that underscores the divergence between the resilient US economy and a Europe absorbing a more direct energy shock.
Crypto Market Implications
For Bitcoin and crypto markets, Thursday's session delivered a mixed but broadly constructive signal. S&P 500 and Nasdaq all-time highs reduce the risk of a broader equity-driven risk-off move that could drag crypto lower, while the oil price reversal from $126 to $114 eases -- at least temporarily -- one of the key inflationary pressures that has been capping Bitcoin's ability to clear $79,000. Apple's after-hours beat and Alphabet's 10% surge suggest the tech earnings season is broadly supportive of risk appetite heading into Friday's session.

data.title: Riot Blockchain Reports $167 Million in First-Quarter Revenue
data.description: Riot Blockchain has announced its financial results for the first quarter, revealing a revenue of $167 million. According to NS3.AI, the company's expanded data center operations contributed $33.2 million to this figure. Riot Blockchain also disclosed that it currently holds approximately 15,679 Bitcoin.
